title: 7 Reporting to Central 1
body_text:
7	Reporting to Central 1
7.1	Reporting Periods and Submission Deadlines
The first Reporting Period runs from the 1st to the 15th of the month and the second Reporting Period runs from the 16th to the end of the month. On the first three business days following the last day of a Reporting Period, Financial Institutions submit their AgriInvest Activity Files to Central 1. It is recommended that Financial Institutions submit their Activity Files on the first two business days to allow a buffer for error corrections and resubmissions before the end of the third business day.
Central 1 runs file pickups every half hour between 6 am PT and 6 pm PT on the first three business days after each reporting period. The system sends out emails to Financial Institutions after each file pickup to notify if a file was successfully uploaded or if it was rejected. The system also sends out a reminder email at the end of the second business day to Financial Institutions for which Activity Files havenât been received.
The AgriInvest Activity Files are amalgamated into regional files that Central 1 transmits to AAFC. A regional file canât be transmitted to AAFC until the Activity Files from all the Financial Institutions within that region have been received by Central 1.
7.2	Using the Excel Template to Create an AgriInvest Activity File
This Section provides steps on using the AgriInvest Activity File Template, Form 3793, to create the Activity File. All accounts must be reported on the Activity File, even if there is no activity on the account. Each account transaction is entered on a separate line.
Note:	Alternatively, the Activity File can be created using Notepad or WordPad. See AgriInvest Activity File Specifications, Form 3794, for more information.
The AgriInvest Activity File Template, Form 3793, consists of the Layout sheet and the Validation Summary sheet. Financial Institutions enter data in the appropriate cells on the Layout sheet. Pop-up comments on the template provide details on the type of information that is required in each cell. For example, Row A, Column D is the start date of the Reporting Period. The date for this day will always be either 01 or 16. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: AgriInvest Activity File Template, Form 3793, Layout Sheet
Enter account transactions using the transaction types shown in the table below. For further details on the information required for each cell, see AgriInvest Activity File Specifications, Form 3794.
Transaction Type
Sign
Description
Deposit
D
To post a deposit into the AgriInvest Account
(e.g., D 2,500.00).
For a deposit correction, make a reversal entry with a minus in front of the amount (e.g., D -100.00.)
Withdrawal
W
To post a withdrawal from the AgriInvest Account
(e.g., W -100.00).
For a withdrawal correction, make a reversal entry without a minus in front of the amount (e.g., W 100.00).
Interest
I
To post interest to the AgriInvest Account (e.g., I 250.00)
For an interest correction, make a reversal entry with a minus in front of the amount (e.g., I -250.00).
After the required data is entered on the Layout Sheet, select the Validation Summary sheet to cross-check data entries and to check for schema errors. When schema errors are validated, there is less chance of the Activity File being rejected by the Central 1 system. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: AgriInvest Activity File Template, Form 3793, Validation Summary Sheet
Saving the Excel Template as Backup
When all the data has been entered and validated:
Save the Excel file in .xls/.xlsx format, using the current billing period date as the naming convention (AG01MMDD).
Close the file (keep it as a backup copy in case a regeneration is required within the same Reporting Period).
Converting to CSV Format
Open the backup .xls/.xlsx file.
Select
File
>
Save As
.
Update file name to âAG01MMDD.
dat
â format.
Select the drop-down arrow in the
Save as type
field.
Select
CSV
(Comma delimited) (*.csv)
from the list.
Select
OK
and
YES
to any messages that pop up. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Excel to CSV File Conversion
Right-click on the file.
Select
Rename
and delete .csv from the file name (AG01MMDD.dat).
Select
Yes
for the file extension warning.
The Activity File (AG01MMDD.dat) is now ready for uploading to Central 1.
Related Information

Form 3793

7.3	Uploading AgriInvest Activity Files on the Client Centre
Activity Files are uploaded through the File and Report Exchange on the Client Centre or via SFTP. For information on uploading files via the SFTP connection, see the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part.
To upload an Activity File through the File and Report Exchange:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 4.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 4: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
, then select
Production Applications
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
File and Report Exchange
. See Figure 6.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youâve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under âMy Favouritesâ.
Figure 6: Accessing File and Report Exchange
Double-click on the Agrinvest folder to open it. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Opening the Agrinvest Folder
Select
Upload
. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Preparing to Upload Activity File
Select
Browse
and navigate to the Activity File. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Browsing for the Activity File
When the file name appears in the File Path, select
Upload
.
When the upload is complete, select
Close
. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Uploading the Selected Activity File
The file is uploaded to the Agrinvest folder and ready for pickup by the Central 1 system. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Activity File Ready for Pickup
7.4	Central 1 Validation Process and Notifications
File pickup times are scheduled every half hour between 6 am PT and 6 pm PT on the first three business days after each reporting period. During the validation process after each file pickup, the Central 1 system sends an email to the Financial Institution on the status of the submitted Activity File. One of the following notifications is sent:
validation was successful â no further action is required from the Financial Institution;
file was rejected (e.g., incorrect Reporting Period date); or
account or transaction was rejected (e.g., incorrect date format or other schema errors).
Note:	If the Financial Institution doesnât receive an email notification, check the format of the file that was submitted. The file must be submitted in csv format (i.e., AG01MMDD
.dat
) for it to be recognized by the Central 1 system.
When a file is rejected, the Financial Institution can correct the error in the backup Excel file or in the csv data file and resubmit the Activity File, using the same file name.
Important:
If making corrections using the Excel backup file, make the correction, save the file in .xls/.xlsx format, and then follow the steps provided to convert the file to csv format. See Section 7.2, Using the Excel Template to Create an Activity File, Subsection, Converting to CSV Format.
The system sends out a reminder email to Financial Institutions for which Activity Files havenât been picked up by 6 pm PT on the second business day of the Reporting Period.
7.5	Processing Error Corrections
The Central 1 system picks up file errors or schema errors and notifies Financial Institutions of such errors shortly after file pickup. However, the Central 1 system doesnât detect missing accounts or transactions or incorrect transaction entries. These types of errors are detected by AAFC Administration and communicated to the Central 1 AgriInvest Officer who acts as a liaison between the Financial Institution and AAFC. The Financial Institution can submit corrections to errors detected by AAFC in the next Reporting Period.
Note:	The original transaction date is the date a transaction is processed to the memberâs AgriInvest account (Excel > Column E in the D Record line). The transaction entry date is the date a transaction is entered on the AgriInvest Activity File and posted to the banking system (Excel > Column D in the D Record line).
Example 1
A Participant makes a withdrawal on the AgriInvest account on May 25. The transaction isnât reported in the June 1 reporting period. The oversight is detected and the transaction is entered in the June 16 reporting period. In this scenario, the original transaction date is May 25. The entry date is the date on which the transaction is actually posted to the system (e.g., June 16, 2016).
Scenario
Action if upload deadline NOT passed
Action if upload deadline passed
If your file was rejected (e.g., incorrect Reporting Period date, etc.)
Make the necessary corrections in the Excel backup file or in the csv data file and upload the corrected Activity File.
N/A
If an account or transaction was rejected (e.g., incorrect date format or other schema errors)
Make the necessary corrections in the Excel backup file or in the csv data file and upload the corrected Activity File.
N/A
If you forgot to include a transaction.
Make the necessary corrections in the Excel backup file or in the csv data file and upload the corrected Activity File.
File the missing transactions and submit in the next Reporting Period cycle.
Note: In this scenario, the original transaction date will be outside of the current reporting period. The entry date is the date the transaction is entered on the AgriInvest Activity File and posted to the system.
If you created incorrect transactions (detected by AAFC).
N/A
In the next Reporting Period cycle, create reversing entries for the incorrect transactions.
Note: In this scenario, the original transaction date is the date the correction is made to the AgriInvest account. The entry date is the date the correction is entered on the AgriInvest Activity File and posted to the system.
Financial Institution doesnât receive an email after uploading the Activity File.
Check the file format of the submitted Activity File. The format must be AG01MMDD.dat.
N/A
Financial Institution is unable to upload Activity File due to an emergency situation.
Contact the Central 1 AgriInvest Officer at
agriinvest@central1.com
and advise of the issue.
END
